concerns.text: The Independent Test references 'the [deferred] interval' without defining what metric or reference this interval represents. While [deferred] is a sanctioned marker for empirical values, the text should clarify that this refers to the 95% confidence interval of the null distribution to ensure the test is actionable.

Acceptance Scenarios 2 and 3 use '[deferred]' to describe the position of the observed value relative to the null distribution (e.g., 'top [deferred] of the null distribution'). This is ambiguous; it should explicitly state 'top 5%' or 'outside the 95% confidence interval' to match the p < 0.05 requirement in the same sentence, ensuring the test criteria are precise.

The definition of 'Null Distribution' states it is generated from '[deferred] permuted versions'. This is a misuse of the [deferred] marker. The number of permutations (2,000) is a fixed requirement defined in FR-003 and User Story 1, not a deferred empirical value. The text should read '2,000 permuted versions'.

The assumption states the permutation count will be reduced to '[deferred]' as a fallback. This is a scope shift; the fallback value should be a specific number (e.g., 500 or 1,000) or a specific condition (e.g., 'until runtime < 6 hours'), not a placeholder marker, to ensure the requirement is testable and the scope is stable.
